meta.maintenanceInstruction: KEEP THE PLAN UP TO DATE AS YOU EVOLVE THE CODE. DEVIATIONS TO THE PLAN MUST BE LOGGED WITHIN THE PLAN. THE PLAN MUST BE REASSESSED HOLISTICALLY AFTER ANY CHANGE OF ANY SIZE.
meta.completionPolicy: YOU ARE NOT DONE UNTIL YOU HAVE COMPLETED 100% OF THE BLUEPRINT. DO NOT STOP. YOU MUST REFINE AT REGULAR INTERVALS TO KEEP THE BLUEPRINT FULL. NEVER SPECIFY ANY ESTIMATE OF EFFORT OR COST — ESPECIALLY NOT TIME ESTIMATES. NEVER SPECIFY A PRIORITY OF ANY KIND.

globalAlerts.warning: ALL checks must pass on ALL platforms (ubuntu-latest, windows-latest, macos-latest). Use `runtime.GOOS == "windows"` and `runtime.GOOS != "windows"` skips where needed. Never accept failing or flaky tests.

TDD ENFORCEMENT: The implementing agent MUST author comprehensive FAILING tests FIRST for each defect, THEN implement the fix. Tests must prove the defect exists (red) before the fix makes them pass (green). No implementing without tests first.

GNU Make is the build system. Use `make test`, `make lint`, `make build`. On macOS use `gmake` or confirm `make` is GNU Make. Single-package tests: `go test ./internal/scripting/... -run TestName -v`.
Tests must not mutate host system state. Use t.TempDir(), t.Setenv(), and mock external services. Never assume a specific home directory.

mandatoryDirectives.contextManagement: Tasks are grouped to be executed in manageable units. Each task covers one cohesive defect with its failing tests first, then the source fix. Do not combine multiple unrelated fixes into one task.
mandatoryDirectives.tddWorkflow: For EVERY defect: (1) Write failing test(s) that reproduce the defect, (2) Verify tests fail with `go test ./internal/scripting/... -run <TestName>`, (3) Implement the fix, (4) Verify tests pass, (5) Run full suite `make test && make lint` before marking Done.

statusSection.currentState: Two reviewers identified 3 correctness blockers in the tilde-expansion / path-resolution code. No fixes have been started. The PR is blocked until all issues are resolved.
statusSection.goalState: All three blockers are fixed with comprehensive passing tests: (1) ExpandTilde rejects non-absolute home directories, (2) findOwnerFromUserPath uses host-specific canonicalization BEFORE cross-platform tilde fallback so RemovePath/RefreshPath remain symmetric with AddPath on POSIX for literal ~\ paths, (3) AddRelativePath on POSIX with ~\ input tries literal resolution before home-relative expansion to avoid wrong-target rehydration. TestAddPath_LiteralBackslashTildeOnPOSIX is fixed to exercise the actual relative ~\ input path. Full `make test && make lint` passes on all platforms.

sequentialTasks.task: Write failing test: ExpandTilde rejects non-absolute home directory
sequentialTasks.description: Add a test to `internal/filepathutil/expand_tilde_test.go` that sets the injectable `userHomeDir` var to return a relative path (e.g. "relative-home") then calls `ExpandTilde("~/x")`. Assert it returns an error containing "not absolute" or similar. This test MUST fail against current code because `ExpandTilde` at `internal/filepathutil/expand_tilde.go:50-60` concatenates `home + separator + path[2:]` without checking `filepath.IsAbs(home)`. Use the existing test pattern of assigning to the package-level `userHomeDir` var and restoring it with defer.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A new test `TestExpandTilde_RelativeHomeErrors` in `internal/filepathutil/expand_tilde_test.go` that compiles and produces a FAILING result when run with `go test ./internal/filepathutil/... -run TestExpandTilde_RelativeHomeErrors`. The test demonstrates that `ExpandTilde` currently silently accepts a relative home directory and returns a relative path instead of erroring.

sequentialTasks.task: Implement fix: ExpandTilde rejects non-absolute home directory
sequentialTasks.description: In `internal/filepathutil/expand_tilde.go`, inside `ExpandTilde()`, after `home, err := userHomeDir()` and the error check (around line 51-53), add: `if !filepath.IsAbs(home) { return "", fmt.Errorf("home directory for tilde expansion is not absolute: %q", home) }`. This makes the function's documented contract (tilde-expanded paths are inherently absolute) actually enforced. All existing callers (`canonicalizeUserPath`, `expandTildeOwnerLabel`, `internal/builtin/os.expandTildeOnly`) benefit immediately. Verify `TestExpandTilde_RelativeHomeErrors` now passes (green).
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `TestExpandTilde_RelativeHomeErrors` transitions from red to green. ALL existing tests in `internal/filepathutil/expand_tilde_test.go` still pass. `make test ./internal/filepathutil/...` passes. The error message explicitly identifies the problem as a non-absolute home directory.

sequentialTasks.task: Write failing test: AddRelativePath with ~\ input on POSIX rejects relative home instead of rebasing onto basePath
sequentialTasks.description: Add a test to `internal/scripting/context_paths_test.go` (POSIX-only, skip on windows) that: sets `userHomeDir` (via `filepathutil` package-level var — may need to export a test-only setter or use the existing test seam) to return a relative path like "relative-home", then calls `AddRelativePath("~/x")`. Assert it returns an error (not a successful tracking that silently resolved relative to basePath). If the `userHomeDir` var is unexported in filepathutil, use `t.Setenv("HOME", "relative-home")` as the test seam — but note from the code comments that `os.UserHomeDir` has OS-level fallbacks. The test must demonstrate the `ExpandTilde` guard from Task 2 propagates correctly through `AddRelativePath` -> `expandTildeOwnerLabel` -> `filepathutil.ExpandTilde`.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A POSIX-only test in `internal/scripting/context_paths_test.go` named `TestAddRelativePath_RelativeHomeErrors` that compiles and FAILS, proving that the current code allows `AddRelativePath` to silently track a basePath-relative path when home is non-absolute instead of erroring.

sequentialTasks.task: Verify AddRelativePath relative-home fix propagates from Task 2
sequentialTasks.description: This task is verification-only — no new code changes. The fix from Task 2 (ExpandTilde rejecting non-absolute home) should propagate up through `expandTildeOwnerLabel` -> `AddRelativePath` -> `isAbsolute` in builtin/os. Run `go test ./internal/scripting/... -run TestAddRelativePath_RelativeHomeErrors` and confirm it passes. If it doesn't, the propagation chain needs an additional fix in `expandTildeOwnerLabel` or `AddRelativePath` to properly bubble the error.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `TestAddRelativePath_RelativeHomeErrors` passes. `make test ./internal/scripting/...` passes. No other tests break.

sequentialTasks.task: Write failing test: RemovePath with relative ~\ input on POSIX is symmetric with AddPath
sequentialTasks.description: Add a test to `internal/scripting/context_paths_test.go` (POSIX-only, skip on windows) that: (1) Creates a temp base dir and a separate temp cwd dir. (2) Creates a literal directory named `~\docs` under cwd (not base). (3) Creates a file `notes.txt` inside that directory. (4) Changes process CWD to cwd (save/restore with defer). (5) Creates a ContextManager with basePath=base. (6) Calls `cm.AddPath(filepath.Join("~\\docs", "notes.txt"))` — this is the relative `~\docs/notes.txt` input that `canonicalizeUserPath` should resolve to `cwd/~\docs/notes.txt` (host-specific, no tilde expansion on POSIX). (7) Assert AddPath succeeded. (8) Calls `cm.RemovePath(filepath.Join("~\\docs", "notes.txt"))` with the SAME input string. (9) Assert RemovePath succeeded. (10) Assert the path is no longer tracked via `cm.GetPath`. This test MUST fail against current code because `findOwnerFromUserPath` step 3 uses cross-platform `expandTildeOwnerLabel` which reinterprets `~\docs` as `$HOME/docs`, producing a different path than what `AddPath` stored.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A POSIX-only test `TestRemovePath_LiteralBackslashTildeRelativeOnPOSIX` that compiles and FAILS, demonstrating the AddPath/RemovePath asymmetry for relative `~\` paths when basePath != cwd.

sequentialTasks.task: Write failing test: RefreshPath with relative ~\ input on POSIX is symmetric with AddPath
sequentialTasks.description: Same setup as Task 5 but tests RefreshPath instead of RemovePath. After AddPath succeeds, modify the file content on disk, call `cm.RefreshPath(filepath.Join("~\\docs", "notes.txt"))`, and verify the content was updated (proving RefreshPath found the correct owner). This test MUST fail against current code for the same reason: `findOwnerFromUserPath` step 3 misinterprets the literal `~\` path via cross-platform expansion.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A POSIX-only test `TestRefreshPath_LiteralBackslashTildeRelativeOnPOSIX` that compiles and FAILS, demonstrating the AddPath/RefreshPath asymmetry.

sequentialTasks.task: Write failing test: Wrong-owner avoidance — literal ~\ path vs home-backed ~\ path cannot collide
sequentialTasks.description: POSIX-only test. Setup: (1) Create fakeHome temp dir and set HOME to it. (2) Create a file at `<fakeHome>/docs/notes.txt`. (3) Create a separate cwd temp dir, create literal directory `<cwd>/~\docs`, create file `<cwd>/~\docs/notes.txt`. (4) CWD = cwd, basePath = separate base dir. (5) Call `cm.AddPath("~\\docs/notes.txt")` — on POSIX this resolves to the literal path under cwd. (6) Call `cm.AddRelativePath("~\\docs\\notes.txt")` — this uses cross-platform tilde expansion, resolves to `<fakeHome>/docs/notes.txt`. (7) Both should be tracked as separate entries. (8) Call `cm.RemovePath("~\\docs/notes.txt")` — this MUST remove the literal one (the AddPath entry), NOT the home-backed one (the AddRelativePath entry). (9) Verify the home-backed entry is still tracked. This test MUST fail against current code because `findOwnerFromUserPath` step 3 may resolve to the home-backed owner instead of the literal one.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A POSIX-only test `TestRemovePath_LiteralVsHomeBackslashTildeNoCollision` that compiles and FAILS, demonstrating that RemovePath can target the wrong tracked path when both a literal `~\` entry and a home-backed `~\` entry exist.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix findOwnerFromUserPath: add host-specific canonicalization step before cross-platform tilde fallback
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Tasks 5, 6, and 7 tests all transition from red to green. `findOwnerFromUserPath` resolution order is now: (1) exact match, (2) basePath-relative, (2.5) host-specific canonicalization, (3) cross-platform tilde fallback. On POSIX with input `~\docs/notes.txt`, step 2.5 finds the literal owner before step 3 can misinterpret it as `$HOME/docs/notes.txt`.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix AddRelativePath: literal-first resolution for ~\ inputs on POSIX before home-relative expansion
sequentialTasks.description:
In `internal/scripting/context.go`, modify `AddRelativePath` at lines ~310-315 where it checks `isTildeOwnerLabel(ownerPath)` and unconditionally expands via `expandTildeOwnerLabel`. Add a literal-first check: before expanding to home-relative, try resolving the path literally (as basePath-relative) and if it exists on disk, use that. Only fall through to cross-platform tilde expansion if the literal resolution doesn't find an existing file.

The logic change is in the block starting at `if isTildeOwnerLabel(ownerPath) {` (around line 313):
1. Try literal resolution first: `absPath := filepath.Join(cm.basePath, ownerPath)` with appropriate normalization. If `os.Lstat(absPath)` succeeds, use this path (the literal interpretation).
2. If literal resolution fails (file doesn't exist), fall through to the existing `expandTildeOwnerLabel` cross-platform expansion.
3. If BOTH literal and home-relative exist, this is ambiguous — but we should prefer the literal interpretation since it was likely added that way originally. Log a warning if possible, but don't error.

This addresses Reviewer 2 Blocker 1 — the rehydration scenario where a POSIX literal `~\Documents\file.txt` label gets incorrectly re-expanded as `$HOME/Documents/file.txt`.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: On POSIX, if a real file exists at `<basePath>/~\docs/notes.txt`, then `AddRelativePath("~\\docs\\notes.txt")` tracks the literal file, NOT `$HOME/docs/notes.txt`. The cross-platform tilde fallback still works for Windows-originated labels when no literal file exists. A new test `TestAddRelativePath_LiteralBackslashTildeOnPOSIX` passes.

sequentialTasks.task: Write failing test: AddRelativePath literal ~\ rehydration on POSIX preserves literal path
sequentialTasks.description: POSIX-only test in `internal/scripting/context_paths_test.go`. Setup: (1) Create base temp dir. (2) Create literal directory `<base>/~\docs` and file `<base>/~\docs/notes.txt`. (3) Create ContextManager with basePath=base. (4) Call `cm.AddRelativePath("~\\docs\\notes.txt")`. (5) Verify the file is tracked as the literal path under base (the normalized owner key should contain `~\docs`), NOT as `$HOME/docs/notes.txt`. (6) Verify content matches. This test MUST fail because current `AddRelativePath` unconditionally expands `~\` to home via `expandTildeOwnerLabel`.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: A POSIX-only test `TestAddRelativePath_LiteralBackslashTildeOnPOSIX` that compiles and FAILS, proving that rehydrating a POSIX literal `~\` path via `AddRelativePath` currently targets the wrong file.

sequentialTasks.task: Fix TestAddPath_LiteralBackslashTildeOnPOSIX to exercise relative ~\ input
sequentialTasks.description: The existing test at `internal/scripting/context_paths_test.go` line ~1322 creates an absolute path and passes it to `AddPath`. Per Reviewer 1 issue #2: this does NOT exercise the actual behavior in question because the absolute path no longer begins with `~`. Fix the test to: (1) Create a separate cwd dir, (2) Create `<cwd>/~\docs/notes.txt`, (3) `os.Chdir(cwd)`, (4) Call `cm.AddPath(filepath.Join("~\\docs", "notes.txt"))` with the RELATIVE input, (5) Verify the tracked owner key contains the literal `~\docs` segment, (6) Verify `GetPath` with the relative input still works, (7) Save/restore original CWD with defer. The test should pass after the findOwnerFromUserPath fix from Task 8 is in place.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `TestAddPath_LiteralBackslashTildeOnPOSIX` now calls AddPath with a relative `~\docs/notes.txt` input (not an absolute path). The test proves that on POSIX, AddPath tracks the literal-backslash path end-to-end, and GetPath can retrieve it with the same relative input. Test passes.

sequentialTasks.task: Run full test suite and lint across all affected packages
sequentialTasks.description:
Execute: `make test` and `make lint`. Specifically verify:
- `go test ./internal/filepathutil/... -v` — all ExpandTilde tests pass including new relative-home guard
- `go test ./internal/scripting/... -v` — all context manager tests pass including new symmetry/anti-collision tests
- `go test ./internal/builtin/os/... -v` — os module still works with tilde expansion
- `make lint` — no new vet/staticcheck/deadcode issues

If any test fails, DO NOT mark this task done. Fix the issue and re-run from the failing task.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: `make test` exits 0. `make lint` exits 0. All new tests pass. All pre-existing tests still pass. No regressions.

sequentialTasks.task: Verify test coverage for all modified functions
sequentialTasks.description:
Run `go test -cover ./internal/filepathutil/... ./internal/scripting/...` and inspect coverage. Ensure that the following functions/paths have test coverage:
- `ExpandTilde` relative-home error branch
- `findOwnerFromUserPath` new step 2.5 (host-specific canonicalization)
- `AddRelativePath` literal-first ~\ resolution on POSIX
- `RemovePath` with ~\ relative input on POSIX (symmetry)
- `RefreshPath` with ~\ relative input on POSIX (symmetry)
Add additional tests if any of these paths lack coverage.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Coverage report shows the new error/taken branches in ExpandTilde, findOwnerFromUserPath step 2.5, and AddRelativePath literal-first are all exercised by tests. No untested new code paths remain.

sequentialTasks.task: Clean up scratch/ review files and temporary artifacts
sequentialTasks.description: Remove `scratch/review-1.md` and `scratch/review-2.md` as they are review artifacts that have been fully processed into the blueprint. Ensure no other temporary files were created during the fix process (check for any `.orig`, `.bak`, or scratch files). Leave `scratch/.gitkeep` intact.
sequentialTasks.acceptance: Only `.gitkeep` remains in `scratch/`. No temporary build artifacts or backup files exist in the workspace root.

continuousVerification.task: Run project-defined build and test commands after EVERY significant change
continuousVerification.description:
Execute `make test` and `make lint` after: (a) Each new failing test, (b) Each source fix, (c) Each task completion. Specifically:
- `go test ./internal/filepathutil/... -v` after Tasks 1-2
- `go test ./internal/scripting/... -v` after Tasks 3-11
- `make test && make lint` after Tasks 12-13
continuousVerification.failPolicy: If ANY test fails, STOP IMMEDIATELY and fix the issue before proceeding. Zero tolerance for test failures, timing issues, non-determinism, or race conditions. Tests using t.Skip for platform incompatibility are acceptable.
continuousVerification.testCoverage: Verify coverage for modified functions after Task 13. Aim for 100% effective coverage on new code paths.

finalEnforcementProtocol.alert: ALL platforms must pass. The ~\ tilde behavior differs between POSIX and Windows by design. Every test touching tilde expansion MUST have appropriate GOOS guards. Never remove a GOOS skip.

TDD: tests first, fix second. ExpandTilde absolute-home guard is the foundation — it makes all downstream behavior safer. findOwnerFromUserPath step ordering is the critical fix for AddPath/RemovePath/RefreshPath symmetry. AddRelativePath literal-first fallback is the fix for rehydration correctness.
